basics.name: John Inston
basics.label: PhD Student
basics.summary: A 3rd year PhD student in the Department of Statistics and Applied Probability at UC Santa Barbara.

courses: Statistical Methods for Financial and Economic Applications
Assessing Financial Risk: Extreme Value Methods

work.name: UC Santa Barbara
work.summary: Primary instructor for the courses listed below with responsibilities including course structuring, material preparation, lecturing and grading.

work.name: UC Santa Barbara
work.summary: Assisting primary instructor for the courses listed below with responsibilities including holding section and grading.

awards.title: Award for Best Academic Performance
awards.summary: Awarded in recognition of exceptional academic performance in obtaining the highest GPA on the MSc Quantitative Finance program at Lancaster University in the 2020/21 academic year.

awards.title: Award for Best Dissertation Results
awards.summary: Awarded in recognition of obtaining the highest dissertation results in the MSc Quantitative Finance program at Lancaster University in the 2020/21 academic year.

awards.title: Lancaster Gold Award
awards.summary: Awarded in recognition of skills obtained in activities outside of academia whilst at university. These include: (1) Digital Skills Certificate, (2) Charity Work, (3) Work Experience, and (4) Student Academic Representative.

publications.name: Measuring and Forecasting Mutual Fund Survivial Capacity using Machine Learning Algorithms
publications.summary: My masters dissertation written under the supervision of Dr George Wang at Lancaster University Management School and Prof. Ian D'Souza at NYU Stern Business School.

volunteer.summary: Worked weekends as a volunteer ranger at the National Trust’s estate at Marsden Moor, a large expanse of moorland in the South Pennines, England.
Fundraising and volunteering for the Whale & Dolphin Conservation.

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
